etymology.description: semantic 力(力) (lì, "strength") + phonetic 重(重) (zhòng) -> movement aided by strength -> move
etymology.interpretation: The character 動 suggests using strength 力(力) (lì, "strength") to move something heavy, as indicated by the phonetic component 重(重) (zhòng, "heavy"). This conveys the concept of movement or change.
etymology.simplification: The character 動 was simplified by replacing 重(重) (zhòng, "heavy") with 云(云) (yún, "cloud") to form 动, reducing the complexity of writing.

examples.chinese: 他动了一下椅子(他動了一下椅子)。
我的心被她的话感动了(我的心被她的話感動了)。
examples.pinyin: Wǒ de xīn bèi tā de huà gǎn dòng le.
examples.english: My heart was moved by her words.

examples.chinese: 天气一变动(天氣一變動)，就会下雨(就會下雨)。
examples.pinyin: Tiānqì yī biàn dòng, jiù huì xià yǔ.
examples.english: As soon as the weather changes, it will rain.
